summary: The user flagged that the full 24B corpus estimate is too large relative to available drive capacity.
The user suggested a possible pivot to a 9B model because the 24B storage target may be unrealistic.
The assistant clarified that the dashboard should separate minimum tuning size from full corpus ambition more clearly.
The assistant estimated that a 9B model would reduce the full corpus target to about 670.55 GB and the minimum tuning threshold to about 3.35 GB.
captured_user_directives: Make the distinction between minimum tuning size and full target much more obvious.
Consider scaling the target down to 9B because 24B may be too large for the available storage.
captured_assistant_responses: The current panel uses a pretraining-scale assumption and should relabel the size tiers more clearly.
A 9B target would materially reduce both full target and minimum tuning-start estimates.
